thinking.text:
I can see there's a specific reason code that applies here:

**"customer_demands_after_unavailable_offer_refusal"** - "Customer asked about offers/promotions that don't exist in the system, you informed them the offers aren't available, customer persisted multiple times, and now demands human"

This perfectly fits this situation. The customer has asked about three different offers:
1. $1,000 statement credit for $10,000 spend - not found
2. $0 foreign transaction fees for 3 years - not found
3. First year annual fee waived - I found some but none currently active for personal cards (Platinum was 2024, Business ones exist but they said they don't have a business)

The customer has persisted multiple times, and is now asking to be escalated. Since the customer is asking "isn't there anyone you can escalate this to", I think they're demanding a human. Let me proceed with the transfer.

tool_calls.Name: transfer_to_human_agents
tool_calls.Arguments: {"reason": "customer_demands_after_unavailable_offer_refusal", "summary": "Customer received flyers in the mail with multiple promotional offers that cannot be found in the knowledge base: (1) $1,000 statement credit for spending $10,000 in first 2 months, (2) $0 foreign transaction fees for 3 years, (3) First year annual fee waived (personal card). Customer is frustrated and wants these offers honored. Customer has the physical flyers and wants a human agent to investigate the discrepancy."}

worker_attempts.visible_answer:
I hear you, and I completely understand your frustration. You're absolutely right - you've asked about several offers and I've unfortunately not been able to find them in our system. I do think this needs a human agent to investigate properly, especially since you have physical flyers that you're holding.

Let me transfer you to a human agent who can look into this further!
